author: L .
msg:
#AMA 

•What is the conservative monthly gross incentive estimate for a license exclusively running passive WiFi tasks?

I imagine this would be lowest outcome of all the possibilities that unity will eventually have available.  

We acknowledge the team has a wealth of knowledge that the rest of us are not privy to yet.  Given the prolonged testing period prior to the Unity app & node sales going live, I imagine you would be able to provide a realistic figure here.  

If not now, please provide for the next AMA, as I think this would help UNOs have more of a realistic “bottom line” for incentive potential.

Thanks again team!

author: Zolee
msg:
#amaquestion 
It seems that since recalibration started, decent rewards are only being paid if uptimes are 90%+ 

Anything below that earns 1-2 cents. 
There doesn’t seem to be a middle ground where users earn in relation to their uptime anymore. 

If phones perform telemetry tests throughout the day, earnings should be more in a range, and in line with uptime percentage. 
Concrete example of one of my licenses: 
Uptime 99% -> 46 cents earned. 
Uptime 66% -> 2 cents earned. 

Something changed in the rewards system. 

Can you explain how earnings are calculated for telemetry?

author: Flippy
msg:
The uptime requirement set by UNO is just a place holder. The real uptime requirement for max rewards has never been explained or shared.

From what I have gathered, I see following results for single devices.

Top Tier rewards: 
95% uptime and higher *consistently* (0.20 to 0.25USD)

Mid Tier rewards: 
85% to 95% uptime *consistently* (approx 50% lower rewards than Top tier)

Low Tier rewards: 
65% to 85% uptime *consistently* (approx 50% lower rewards than mid Tier)

Bottom tier: 
Below 65% uptime (rewards near zero)

author: Sophie | I will never DM you first
msg:
Have you tested it? 
FYI: ULO Network Evaluation Toolkit - monitor your ULO quality 24/7 with full visibility into device grading, network quality, connectivity, device type and more. See exactly how each of your devices is rated and understand why your rewards look the way they do. The toolkit will highlight setups that may be classified as synthetic networking so you can see at a glance what's performing well for current tasks and what isn't. Different customers pay for different things, and different tasks value different setups - this tool gives you the transparency to optimize accordingly. Identify issues, improve your network, and track your performance in one place.

author: Janilom81
msg:
I think we all know by now that companies are paying for services provided by Unity (ULOs). To me, this means that automatically things are not always going to remain the same which includes reward amounts.

Until we have loads of tasks running on the network, the low rewards will be felt more by ULOs.

author: GliTch_
msg: Logic, and previous encounters to this ongoing timeline. We can tell by the rewards how they are rated that doesn't tell us when or why it has changed which is what people were asking from the point that the rewards changed. Building a tool that tells us the same thing without knowing if the devices are properly being flagged for those poor results is what people are looking for.

author: Adam Buracchi
msg:
#amaquestion 
#ama

@Kyle_FrenOne @defiants_kay 
 
Just amended my ama questions to reflect recents updates. 

Is the team aware of how disconnected things feel from the community’s perspective?

1)  Q1 roadmap items not completed 
2) The CLI volume of calls is increasing so I won’t say anything about it… 

3) SMS tasks non existent still 
4) R1 is still active — can you clarify why?
5) New WiFi tasks have been coming soon in 7–10 days since January! 
6) It’s unclear whether there are active clients at this stage

At the moment, communication feels very limited, and the community is largely in the dark about what’s actually happening.

Some communication like Jamie did about S&R was absolutely great no messing around no false timelines would be appreciated- the community don’t need much 👍

Many of us have invested significant time and money into growing the edge network. At the very least, we’re asking for transparency and an honest update on the current situation.
Many Thanks 🙏
